title: Our Trade-in quality performance indicators and how they work
bodyHtml:
KPIs are calculated based on items received in the last 30 days. You must be below the average of all Back Market sellers for each one. Here are the quality KPIs that are monitored on the Trade-in quality dashboard:
 

  • Late processing rate (LPR)

This KPI highlights the percentage of orders you did not process on time (i.e. within 48 hours of receiving it).
Calculation: Number of unprocessed orders after 48 hours / number of orders received (in the last 30 days prior to the observation date) that were received more than 2 days ago
 

  • Payment rate 

This KPI gives you a global vision of your quality by comparing the gross merchandise volume (GMV) you actually paid over the GMV promised. 
Calculation: Paid GMV/Promised GMV = the initial price for all orders received
 

  • Counteroffer rate

This KPI is related to the number of counteroffers given.
Calculation: Number of counteroffers/number of orders received in the last 30 days prior to the observation date
 

  • Cancellation rate

This KPI is related to the number of counteroffers that have been refused.
Calculation: Number of canceled orders/number of orders received in the last 30 days prior to the observation date
 

  • Late response rate

This KPI indicates the percentage of transactions where you didn’t respond within 24 hours.

This can include 1 or more of the following:

  • You didn’t complete an action such as confirming payment or any other action under “See more options”
  • You didn’t answer the Back Market customer directly in the chat

How do we calculate this? We divide the number of orders with at least 1 late response by the total number of orders that involved a discussion between you and the Back Market customer.
 

  • On-hold rate

This KPI highlights the percentage of orders that still have the status on-hold in your Back Office.

Calculation: Number of orders on-hold / number of orders received in the last 30 days prior to the observation date.

 

  • STAR rate

The Satisfaction Ticket After Resolution (STAR) rate is a KPI that measures customer satisfaction after Trade-in interactions.

STAR rate is then calculated by dividing the number of positive survey responses by the total number of survey responses in the last 30 days before the observation date.
